title: DeepInit project config (.ai/deepinit.config)
description: Optional per-project settings for DeepInit. Keys are the long-flag names without the leading '--'. Precedence (later wins): built-in max-quality defaults -> this file -> inline flags/mode words. Every key is optional; omitted keys keep the max-quality default. Unknown keys are warned-and-ignored, never fatal (R8) — so additional keys are permitted here for forward/back compatibility.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema so your editor autocompletes + validates the file. DeepInit drops a copy at .ai/deepinit.config.schema.json each run, so "./deepinit.config.schema.json" resolves offline.

depth.description: File-read breadth. deep = deepest per-file (default); thorough = read all files; fast = grep-first heuristic.

review.description: Review cycles: fast = 0; thorough (default) = 2, with a 3rd added automatically only if the cycle-2 quality gate still fails (unresolved CRITICAL or below-target coverage). No knob to force or cap the count — the gate decides. Orthogonal to depth.

components.description: Limit analysis to these components (default: all).

skip-db.description: Skip database analysis even if a DB is detected.

max-lines.description: Lean-tier line budgets only — the deep tier is never capped.

knowledge-log.description: Include the Knowledge Log in the lean root.

include-mermaid.description: Add Mermaid diagrams to the output.

no-graphify.description: Skip Graphify; fall back to a grep-based import/symbol graph.

existing.description: How to handle an existing user-authored context file. extend = owned-region update (default); skip = leave it; replace = overwrite (a dated .bak is always archived first); side-file = write the proposal beside it as CLAUDE.deepinit.md with the original untouched (the 'Preview beside it' option). On a heavy human-authored front-door file with no resolved strategy, Emit asks one plain-language confirmation (recommended = the default).

canonical.description: Which file is the canonical content-bearing lean tier. claude = CLAUDE.md (default, Claude-Code-native; DeepInit owns the front door). agents = AGENTS.md canonical (advanced/AGENTS-first; CLAUDE.md becomes a thin @AGENTS.md import).

emit-agents.description: Force the conditional cross-tool AGENTS.md export + projections even with no cross-tool consumer present.

gitignore-agents.description: Mirror the shared agent file's gitignore state onto the generated CLAUDE.md + cross-tool export.

notify-on-session-start.description: The proactive SessionStart suggestion — when docs are stale, offers a one-click refresh via AskUserQuestion (plugin-shipped hook, 0 tokens, no install).

notify-on-commit.description: Post-commit prints a 0-token staleness nudge in the terminal (deepinit_status.py).

auto-update.description: Opt-in: a detached headless `claude -p` runs --update after a source commit (spends tokens; never auto-commits).

notify-cadence.description: How often the proactive SessionStart suggestion re-fires while docs stay stale. window (default) = at most once per notify-window-hours of wall-clock (≈once/day, so frequent new sessions don't each re-nudge); session = once per NEW session (session-id dedup — proactive but never twice in one session); always = every session start while stale.

notify-window-hours.description: Back-off window in hours for notify-cadence="window" (the default — the SessionStart suggestion fires at most once per this many hours while stale). Ignored for session/always.

notify-snooze-hours.description: How long (hours) a "Not now" decline silences the suggestion. When the user declines the offer the agent records a back-off in .ai/.deepinit-nudge-snooze (via deepinit_status.py --snooze) and the hook stays silent until it expires (default 168 = one week — longer than the passive window, so an explicit decline buys more quiet than letting a nudge pass).

issues.description: Detect issues (report-only; never enters the lean tier — R9).

issues-families.description: Narrow which issue detectors run (default: all shipped — IF-1..IF-5 + IF-8 / IF-3b / IF-7 / IF-6 / IF-10; IF-9 deferred). Zeroes the others' cost term.

issues-min-severity.description: Ledger/SARIF severity floor. The dashboard view defaults to medium+.

issues-baseline.description: Baseline lifecycle: accept current open issues into .issue_baseline.json (write-once-per-id), or reset (clear).

issues-suppress.description: FP-suppression list (a suppressed issue is reported as a named suppression, never silently dropped — R8).

family.description: Issue family to suppress under this path, or "*" for all.

issues-baseline-accept.description: Per-issue baseline match-keys to accept, e.g. "IF-3a:src/cache.rb:redis_key".

report.description: Emit the unified self-contained .ai/report.html (Docs + Insights; supersedes dashboard + viewer — ADR-019).

dashboard.description: Emit the legacy self-contained .ai/dashboard.html (legacy until the report cutover).

viewer.description: Emit the legacy self-contained .ai/docs-viewer.html (legacy until the report cutover).

translate.description: Also emit a translated report.<lang>.html (a post-generation overlay; English report.html stays the canonical analysis output). A shipped language code (es/he), an array of codes, or "other:<language>" for any other language (chrome falls back to English). See references/i18n.md.

heal.description: Governed lifecycle/citation upkeep over --update/--lint. Report-only floor is hardcoded; never touches source.

heal-confidence.description: Raise the confidence bar before `auto` heal acts (loop-stability).

recheck.description: Widen --update-adr recheck to BRs + open issues over the DP-1 blast radius (default: adr only).

max-cost.description: Non-blocking preflight cost ceiling in USD. Proceeds under it; pauses to re-confirm only above it.

yes.description: Suppress every confirmation pause (incl. the over-ceiling cost re-confirm). Canonical form of --no-confirm.
